executives.name: Denise Paulonis
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: Notable enhancements include coupon clarity and loyalty transparency, so value is more visible, easier to interpret, and actionable at the right moments, as well as a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery.
executives.relevance: CEO explicitly names search as a current improvement area — confirms the gap and budget intent

executives.name: Denise Paulonis
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: I'm overwhelmed by choices. TikTok offers me choices. Everybody in the world offers me choices. Help me make the right choice.
executives.relevance: CEO articulating the exact problem Algolia NeuralSearch solves — curation and guided discovery across 18,500+ SKUs

executives.name: Denise Paulonis
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: From our performance marketing, CRM, and personalization initiatives, we are pleased to see expanding customer counts among the millennial and Gen Z cohorts.
executives.relevance: Personalization is already a stated business initiative — Algolia Personalization at the search layer is the missing piece

executives.name: Denise Paulonis
executives.title: President & CEO
executives.quote: Customers acquired through LCOD spend two times more than customers acquired through other means over their first year with us.
executives.relevance: Proves guided discovery drives 2x AOV — Algolia Recommend replicates this at scale across all digital touchpoints

executives.name: Marlo Cormier
executives.title: SVP & CFO
executives.quote: Global ecommerce sales increased 11% to $111 million and represented 12% of total net sales.
executives.relevance: $111M quarterly e-commerce = the revenue base where search optimization delivers immediate ROI

intelligence_signals.title: CEO Names Search Gap on Earnings Call
intelligence_signals.relevance: Direct CEO confirmation of search gap = qualified opportunity
intelligence_signals.text: Denise Paulonis cited 'a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery' as a named enhancement on the Q1 FY2026 earnings call — confirming budget intent and AE entry point.

intelligence_signals.title: BSG Platform Overhaul Spring 2026
intelligence_signals.relevance: Open window to include Algolia in BSG re-platform scope before tech stack is locked
intelligence_signals.text: Sally Beauty's Beauty Systems Group (BSG) is undergoing a substantial app and e-commerce platform update including AI, personalization, and payment modernization — planned Spring 2026.

intelligence_signals.title: Sephora Running Constructor.io for AI Search
intelligence_signals.relevance: First-mover urgency: Sally can leapfrog Sephora's search experience with Algolia NeuralSearch
intelligence_signals.text: Sephora (42.1M monthly visits, Sally's top direct competitor) uses Constructor.io for AI-powered personalized search. Sally runs native SFCC Einstein Search — creating a visible experience gap.

intelligence_signals.title: VP Ecommerce Vacancy + SFCC Engineer Active
intelligence_signals.relevance: Leadership vacancy = new decision authority coming; SFCC engineer = Algolia deployment is technically ready
intelligence_signals.text: Sally Beauty has an active VP Ecommerce opening (leadership gap) and a Salesforce Commerce Cloud Software Engineer posting (deadline Mar 12, 2026) confirming SFCC platform and engineering readiness for Algolia's native cartridge.

intelligence_signals.title: E-Commerce Growing +20% YoY (Sally Segment)
intelligence_signals.relevance: Growing digital channel = greater leverage from search optimization investment
intelligence_signals.text: Sally segment e-commerce grew 20% to $50M in Q1 FY2026; US/Canada e-commerce up 28%. Global e-commerce reached $111M representing 12% of total net sales.

intelligence_signals.title: HQ Layoffs + Relocation (Budget Sensitivity)
intelligence_signals.relevance: Caution: budget sensitivity is real — lead with $5.47M ROI scenario and Fuel for Growth alignment
intelligence_signals.text: Sally Beauty laid off its creative marketing department and is relocating HQ from Denton to Plano, TX. Indicates cost-cutting mode — ROI case must be airtight.

intelligence_signals.title: AI & Personalization Explicitly on BSG Roadmap
intelligence_signals.relevance: AI capability gap is identified at C-suite level — Algolia NeuralSearch is the product that fills it
intelligence_signals.text: CFO described BSG Spring 2026 updates as including 'enhanced capabilities around education, AI, and personalization' — search is the AI layer they don't yet have.

intelligence_signals.title: CosmoProf Institute Digital Education Expansion
intelligence_signals.relevance: Algolia Multi-Index Search uniquely solves this — no single-index search vendor can do products + education natively
intelligence_signals.text: Sally Beauty is expanding CosmoProf Institute online education platform for licensed professionals — creating a new federated search use case (products + education in one search).

intelligence_signals.title: Salesforce SFCC Platform Confirmed (Algolia Native Cartridge Available)
intelligence_signals.relevance: Removes primary technical objection — implementation is plug-and-play with existing SFCC engineer team
intelligence_signals.text: BuiltWith and job postings confirm Salesforce Commerce Cloud as the e-commerce platform. Algolia has a native SFCC cartridge — no custom integration required.

findings.title: Zero NLP / Semantic Understanding
findings.tested_query: best purple shampoo for blonde hair
findings.observation: Query returns keyword-matched results only. Words like 'blonde,' 'best,' and attribute intent ('purple shampoo') are treated as separate tokens, not semantic signals. Natural language product discovery fails completely for Sally's 1,300+ hair color SKUs.
findings.impact_stat: 72% of sites completely fail search expectations
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia NeuralSearch — vector + keyword hybrid understands attribute queries as semantic signals, not token matches
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% conversion rate improvement, +150% sales contribution from search

findings.title: No Federated Search — Content Commerce Gap
findings.observation: Searching 'return policy' returns product results, not help content or FAQ documentation. CosmoProf Institute education content (balayage tutorials, color formulation guides) is not discoverable through the product search experience.
findings.impact_stat: 34% of users attempted to search for non-product content on e-commerce sites
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Multi-Index Federated Search — products, education content, FAQ, and pro-only inventory unified in one results pane
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 150% increase in order rate for new users (Algolia Recommend)

findings.title: No Personalization Layer
findings.observation: A licensed colorist and a first-time DIY shopper searching 'shampoo' see identical results. No segment-based (pro vs. DIY) or individual-user reranking. CQuotient present but limited to rule-based targeting, not real-time user affinity.
findings.impact_stat: 48% of consumers spend more with personalized experiences
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Personalization — real-time user affinity profiles re-rank search results by segment, browse history, and brand affinity
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 150% increase in order rate for new users, 13% higher order rate for returning customers

findings.title: Weak Recommendations on PDP
findings.observation: Product detail page shows limited or no 'Frequently Bought Together' or 'Similar Items.' For a retailer selling complementary products (color + developer + gloves + applicator brush), missing FBT recommendations leaves basket revenue on the table. No dedicated recommendation engine detected in BuiltWith.
findings.impact_stat: On some sites, searchers account for roughly 40% of total revenue
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Recommend — FBT, Similar Items, and Trending models on PDP and post-add-to-cart
findings.algolia_case_study_result: 150% increase in order rate for new users during Black Friday, 32% increase in add-to-cart rate

findings.title: Static Filters — Poor Intent Detection
findings.observation: Filter options appear static regardless of query context. Searching a brand ('CHI') surfaces the same filter set as searching a hair concern ('purple shampoo for blonde hair'). No context-aware facets by hair type, concern, or shade. 18,500 SKU catalog demands category-aware dynamic facets.
findings.impact_stat: 41% of e-commerce sites have critical issues with search query type handling
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Dynamic Faceting — automatically surfaces most relevant filter attributes by query context
findings.algolia_case_study_result: +37% conversion rate improvement, -88% bounce rate reduction

findings.title: Typo Tolerance Gap (Moderate)
findings.observation: Basic single-character typos handled ('shampo' → shampoo results). More complex transpositions ('condtioner') need verification. No visible 'Did You Mean?' correction. Native SFCC typo handling is keyword-level only, without ML-backed fuzzy matching.
findings.impact_stat: 81% of U.S. consumers leave after unsuccessful search
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Typo Tolerance — configurable single/double typo handling, transposition matching, and 'Did You Mean?' UI component
findings.algolia_case_study_result: -88% bounce rate reduction after Algolia implementation

findings.title: SAYT Present (Positive)
findings.observation: Autocomplete dropdown appears with product suggestions. Good foundation to layer Algolia Query Suggestions (trending + recent) and NeuralSearch on top of existing SAYT.
findings.impact_stat: 24% sales and conversion boost from autocomplete
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Suggestions + InstantSearch — enriches SAYT with trending, recent, and AI-powered suggestions

findings.title: Brand Intent Recognition (Positive)
findings.observation: Brand-level intent ('CHI') returns branded products correctly. Good baseline for Algolia Query Rules overlay (brand page redirects, bannering, boosted brand content).
findings.algolia_solution: Algolia Query Rules — extend brand recognition with dedicated brand landing pages, banners, and priority boosts

gap_pairs.quote: a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO explicitly identifies the search efficiency gap. Audit confirms: native SFCC Einstein Search fails semantic/NLP queries — 'best purple shampoo for blonde hair' returns keyword matches only.

gap_pairs.quote: I'm overwhelmed by choices. TikTok offers me choices. Everybody in the world offers me choices. Help me make the right choice.
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO articulates the core customer pain point. Audit confirms: search results are identical for all users regardless of segment or history — no 'choice curation' at the search layer.

gap_pairs.quote: Customers acquired through LCOD spend two times more than customers acquired through other means over their first year with us.
gap_pairs.gap_explanation: CEO proves guided discovery drives 2x AOV. Audit confirms: PDP has no 'Frequently Bought Together' or similar recommendations — the digital equivalent of guided discovery is missing.

revenue_3y.signal: Revenue stabilizing; margins improving (+70 bps EBITDA); Sally Ignited launched

engagement_phases.label: Pre-Hire
engagement_phases.message: Reach out before VP Ecommerce is filled — current digital team open to technology conversations

engagement_phases.message: Demo Algolia SFCC native cartridge — no custom integration; show NeuralSearch live on beauty queries

engagement_phases.timing: Before BSG platform scope is locked (Spring 2026)

objection_counters.objection: We're already on Salesforce — that's enough
objection_counters.counter: SFCC Einstein is basic keyword search. Algolia's SFCC cartridge adds NeuralSearch, Personalization, and Recommend on top without replacing the platform.

objection_counters.objection: Budget is tight with Fuel for Growth
objection_counters.counter: Algolia reduces merchandising labor (automated rules) AND lifts search conversion revenue. It's a net positive on the Fuel for Growth math.

objection_counters.objection: We're in the middle of a BSG platform update
objection_counters.counter: That's the exact window. Adding Algolia now is easier than retrofitting after the platform is locked. What's the RFP timeline?

hiring.build_vs_buy: BUY — Sally's CTO has said 'we deployed cloud-first using the plumbing we already had.' Algolia is that plumbing for search.

strategic_angles.label: Sally Ignited = Search Ignited
strategic_angles.hook: Your Sally Ignited strategy explicitly calls out AI transformation. Algolia NeuralSearch IS the AI-powered search layer that accelerates your website modernization.
pain_points: CEO named 'more efficient search engine' as a current gap on Q1 earnings call
Native SFCC Einstein Search lacks NLP, semantic understanding, and real-time analytics
Sally Ignited timeline matches Algolia implementation window
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Your Sally Ignited strategy specifically calls out AI transformation. How are you currently applying AI to your site search? Are you still on native SFCC Einstein Search?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch — vector + keyword hybrid AI that understands natural language at scale

strategic_angles.label: Beat Sephora on AI Search
strategic_angles.hook: Sephora is running Constructor.io for AI-powered personalized search. Sally is on native SFCC — that's an experience gap your customers can feel.
pain_points: Sephora (42.1M monthly visits) uses Constructor.io — confirmed
Sally (4.75M monthly visits) on native SFCC — confirmed
Neither Ulta nor Sephora uses Algolia — first-mover opportunity
strategic_angles.discovery_question: Sephora uses Constructor for their search — that's who you're competing with. What would it mean for Sally to have search that's on par or better than Sephora's experience?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia NeuralSearch matches and exceeds Constructor.io capabilities with proven beauty retail case studies

strategic_angles.label: Professional + Consumer Federated Search
strategic_angles.hook: Sally serves both DIY consumers and licensed professionals. Algolia can power federated search across products, CosmoProf Institute education, and pro-only inventory.
pain_points: BSG platform update Spring 2026 is live scope
CosmoProf Institute expansion creates new content search use case
B2B (BSG) + B2C (Sally) unified experience requires multi-index search
strategic_angles.discovery_question: When a licensed stylist searches 'balayage' on BSG, should they see products AND education content in the same results? Is that one experience today or two separate ones?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Algolia Multi-Index Federated Search — products, education, FAQ unified in one results pane

strategic_angles.label: Fuel for Growth ROI Story
strategic_angles.hook: With $120M cumulative savings targets, every dollar must prove ROI. Algolia lifts revenue AND reduces merchandising labor — a net Fuel for Growth positive.
pain_points: Yellow EBITDA margin (12.6%) means every conversion improvement flows to bottom line
Current manual merchandising rules are labor-intensive
Search ROI is one of the highest-ROI digital investments available
strategic_angles.discovery_question: With the Fuel for Growth initiative, what's the threshold for a search investment to make sense? If we could show $5M annual lift, would that math work for your business case?
strategic_angles.algolia_proof: Moderate scenario: $5.47M/year at 10% search conversion lift on $54.7M addressable base [ESTIMATE]

priority_to_product.discovery_question: Your Sally Ignited strategy specifically calls out AI transformation. How are you currently applying AI to your site search?
When you say 'enhanced personalization' for BSG, are you thinking segment-level (pro vs. student vs. salon owner) or individual user-level personalization?

priority_to_product.their_priority: a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery
priority_to_product.discovery_question: Denise cited this on the Q1 call — what's on the roadmap to address it? Is this in scope for the BSG update or the Sally app?
If we could show a 10–15% lift in search conversion — roughly $4–8M annually — would that math work for your business case?

outreach_sequence: Step 1: LinkedIn message to Chris Hansen (VP Digital Product) — 'Saw Denise mention search efficiency on your Q1 call...'
Step 2: Technical demo for Scott Lindblom — SFCC native cartridge, no custom integration required
Step 3: ROI workshop with Natalie Lockhart + Marlo Cormier — $5.47M moderate scenario
anchor_lines: Sally Beauty told investors they need 'a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery' — we can show you that in a 30-minute demo.
Sephora runs Constructor for AI search. Sally can leapfrog them with Algolia — and be the lighthouse customer for professional beauty AI search.
Your LCOD customers spend 2x more because they get guided discovery. Algolia Recommend digitizes that at scale across every product page.

competitor_tiers.implication: Sally is already behind — urgency is real
Leapfrog window exists — first to AI search wins
Same gap, same solution — fix both with one implementation

experience_debt.description: Sally runs SFCC native Einstein Search. Sephora runs Constructor AI search. The experience gap is visible and growing.

positioning_statements.vs_constructor: Algolia NeuralSearch matches or exceeds Constructor.io capabilities with proven beauty retail case studies (Lacoste +37% CVR) and a native SFCC cartridge that requires no custom engineering.
positioning_statements.vs_sfcc_native: SFCC Einstein is keyword-only, with no NLP, no vector search, and no dynamic personalization. Algolia adds all three without replacing the platform.

competitive_synthesis.the_ask: 30-minute NeuralSearch demo on Sally's live catalog, showing 'best purple shampoo for blonde hair' before and after Algolia.

golden_angle.headline: First-Mover in Professional Beauty AI Search
golden_angle.detail: No major professional beauty retailer — not Ulta, not CosmoProf, not Bath & Body Works — uses Algolia today. Sally Beauty could be Algolia's lighthouse customer for this $54.7B professional beauty vertical.

ae_fields.urgency_reason: BSG platform update Spring 2026 — scope must be locked imminently. VP Ecommerce vacancy creates decision window.
ae_fields.talk_track_opener: Denise Paulonis named 'a more efficient search engine for easier product discovery' on your Q1 earnings call. We can show that in 30 minutes — want to see it on your live catalog?
ae_fields.cta: 30-min NeuralSearch live demo on sallybeauty.com + BSG catalogs

next_steps.title: Discovery Call — Chris Hansen (VP Digital Product)
next_steps.description: LinkedIn outreach. Lead with Q1 earnings call quote. Ask: 'Is search in scope for the BSG Spring 2026 update, or is the stack already locked?' Most accessible entry point — active on LinkedIn, speaks at CommerceNext.

next_steps.title: Technical Demo — Scott Lindblom (CIO) + SFCC Engineering
next_steps.description: Show Algolia SFCC native cartridge. Demo NeuralSearch on 'best purple shampoo for blonde hair' vs. current experience. Highlight: no rip-and-replace, plug-and-play with existing SFCC engineer team.

next_steps.title: ROI Workshop — Natalie Lockhart + Marlo Cormier
next_steps.description: Model $5.47M moderate scenario against Algolia enterprise contract. Tie to Fuel for Growth targets. Present Lacoste (+37% CVR) and Gymshark (+150% new user order rate) as beauty/apparel benchmarks. Ask for budget approval before BSG scope lock.
